page: Amazon Web Services i8g.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, it features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB per core, and a bundled 3750 GB local NVMe SSD. Network connectivity is supported by a 9 Gbps baseline bandwidth, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the i8g.4xlarge demonstrates strong memory and decompression capabilities. It achieves a top-tier Passmark memory score of 3336.33 and strong large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 296.95 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression reaches 30.33 GB/sec, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs at 202.88 GB/sec. Geekbench scores are strong, with 1887.0 for single-core and 15908.0 for multi-core. However, LLM inference speeds are a notable tradeoff, with text generation on a medium 7B model averaging 16.71 tokens/sec and dropping to a poor 2.29 tokens/sec for a large 70B model.

Given its high local storage density and strong memory performance, the i8g.4xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-speed local data access without the overhead of external storage attachments. The server is well-suited for storage-intensive applications, database operations, and data decompression tasks. It is less optimal for heavy machine learning inference workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services i8g.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by 16 dedicated arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of memory and a bundled 3750 GB local NVMe SSD, operating under the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a 9 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier memory performance, with a Passmark memory score of 3336.33, and strong large-block memory read bandwidth of 296.95 GB/sec. The server also delivers strong database operations and multi-threaded decompression capabilities. However, LLM inference performance is limited, particularly for larger models. This hardware profile is highly efficient for local database hosting, data-intensive storage applications, and high-throughput memory workloads, while avoiding the need for external storage provisioning.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated arm64 Graviton cores, 128 GB of RAM, and a bundled 3750 GB local NVMe SSD for high-throughput database and storage workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.4xlarge is an arm64 storage-optimized server with 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 3750 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized arm64 server featuring dedicated Graviton cores, high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.8 GHz
Bundled 3750 GB local NVMe SSD storage
128.0 GB of system memory with top-tier bandwidth performance
9 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for high-throughput database operations and data decompression
